key_insight: HiReLC uses a two-level reinforcement learning controller to jointly prune and quantize neural networks while allocating compression budgets with sensitivity-aware coordination.
engineering_relevance: The method offers an architecture-agnostic way to reduce model size and preserve accuracy, making it relevant for deploying efficient deep networks on constrained hardware.
method: Low-level agents choose per-block settings for bitwidth, pruning ratio, quantization type, and granularity.
High-level agents coordinate global compression budgets using ensemble voting and Fisher Information sensitivity estimates.
An active-learning loop uses a lightweight surrogate to speed policy search, with post-compression fine-tuning for final validation.
evidence_signal: Across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks, the method achieved about 5.99-6.72x compression with accuracy changes ranging from a 3.83% gain to 0.55-5.62% drops.
watch_item: Surrogate-guided reward shaping can speed search, but final performance still depends on expensive fine-tuning and may be sensitive to proxy quality.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
